body:
This PR removes the use of `CUDA.@allowscalar`, and replaces it with `ClimaComms.allowscalar`.

This is a peel off from #1791.

A step towards #1785 and #1780
title: Use ClimaComms allowscalar

body: This is a peel off from #1791.
title: Reduce CUDA dependency in tests

body:
This PR decomposes our tests into unit, convergence, and simulation tests.

The selected tests were somewhat heavily driven by tests that use `OrdinaryDiffEq`.

As I'm thinking more about this, I think a good naming taxonomy is:

*Correctness tests*
 - Unit tests (high covergage and minimalistic)
 - Convergence tests
 - Simulation tests (e.g., against analytic solutions)

*Performance tests*
 - Inference tests
 - Allocation tests
 - Flop-counting tests

and Benchmarks (which are kind of difficult to benchmark due to noise). I'm going to periodically update the readme as things incubate.

I'm hoping that this PR speeds up `ClimaCore> test` while not hurting coverage too bad.
title: Decompose unit tests into unit, convergence, and simulation tests

body: This PR continues to partition the correctness test into unit, convergence, benchmark etc. I've also cleaned up the log a bit.
title: Partition correctness tests
